relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: At ninety-nine, Abram encountered the Eternal as El Shaddai and was called to walk in wholeness.
The covenant was renewed with overflowing multiplication.
Abram fell on his face as the Divine spoke with him.
The covenant expanded Abram into fatherhood beyond one household, toward a multitude of nations.
Abram became Abraham, his name opened wide to carry many nations.
Fruitfulness, nations, and kings were spoken into his future.
The covenant would move through generations, binding the Divine to Abraham’s seed.
The land of sojourning was promised as enduring possession, and the Divine promised presence with the seed.
Abraham was called to keep the covenant through his generations.
The covenant entered flesh through circumcision, an embodied sign of belonging.
The sign was cut into the body as a visible mark of the bond.
The sign reached the whole male household, revealing the ancient household world in all its complexity.
The covenant in flesh was named everlasting.
To reject the sign was to break the bond and be cut off from the people.
Sarai too was renamed. Sarah entered the covenant by name, not as an afterthought.
The Divine blessed Sarah directly: from her would come son, nations, and kings.
Abraham laughed at the impossible promise, old bodies meeting divine timing.
He asked that Ishmael might live before the Divine.
The covenant line would come through Isaac, the child of Sarah and laughter.
Ishmael was heard and blessed too, destined for fruitfulness and a great nation.
Isaac would carry the covenant at the appointed time through Sarah.
The Divine finished speaking and went up from Abraham.
Abraham acted that very day, marking Ishmael and the whole household with the covenant sign.
Abraham received the sign in his old age.
Ishmael received it at thirteen.
Father and son were marked together on the same day.
The whole male household entered the sign with Abraham.
